text:
MECO Engineering Company, Inc. (MECO) is pleased to present qualifications to the City of Hunnewell for Professional Engineering Services specifically related to Wastewater Treatment Improvements.

OUR FIRM
MECO Engineering is celebrating its 40th anniversary this year. This long-spanning career has been built on serving municipalities, such as yours, in a vast span of projects with varying needs, requirements, funding, and challenges. We take pride in gaining the majority of our business from repeat, satisfied customers.

MECO currently has six office locations, with a location conveniently close in Hannibal, MO, 30 miles from The City of Hunnewell. This, as well as our knowledge of the area and ever-changing regulations, including MODNR funding, and our extensive experience specifically in wastewater collection and treatment, allows us to assure you of our ability to serve the City of Hunnewell with any needs related to wastewater treatment improvements.

MECO's "Standard of Care" and "Project Approach" is a team-centric based philosophy to ensure that every step of project scope development and design review fully invests the client in each step of the process. MECO does not believe in an engineer-driven project development process; we see the most value in investing our client input in the process, first and foremost. Our clients will never see MECO force a project or a solution upon them. Our clients will always lead the initiative for developing projects and working with our professional staff to determine the best suited solutions to their needs.

You can rely on MECO and our Project Team to provide the attention to detail, prompt response, and a level of commitment to service, experience, knowledge, and expertise that you require and expect of your Engineering Consultant. We never forget that clients have many choices and options in their selection process for a consulting engineering firm. We would be honored to be selected to serve the District in this capacity.

MECO currently has 60 professionals with many talents and continues to welcome more team members to our dedicated team as we expand. Our firm is well-positioned and highly qualified to be considered as your consultant and engineer for your selection process based on expertise, knowledge, and experience.

Your specialized team for your project will include:

Robert Hogan, PMP, Senior Engineer, Project Manager – 23 years experience
Max Middendorf, PE, Senior Principal Engineer – 38 years experience
Kevin Garnett, PE, Executive Vice President – 26 years experience
Andy Long, Technical Manager – 32 years experience

This highly qualified team, and the years of projects they bring with them, allow us to assure you of our ability to complete the project in a timely, cost efficient manner that will follow a timeline MECO will work with the city to establish.

MECO was commissioned by the City of Stover to study the existing sanitary sewer collection system and wastewater treatment facilities and to prepare a Preliminary Engineering Report/Facility Plan. Extremely strict effluent limits were imposed by the Missouri Department of Natural Resources for this small rural Morgan County community of less than 1,000, due to their close proximity to a highly sensitive, classified receiving stream.

MECO provided engineering design for wastewater system improvements, as presented in the Preliminary Engineering Report, dated April 2009 (447-006). Project scope components included: abandonment of the existing aerated lagoons, consolidation of all wastewater into one treatment facility, construction of a new wastewater treatment facility, replacement of two existing lift stations with gravity sewer lines, replacement of an existing lift station with the construction of one new lift station in the northeast section of the city, extension of new gravity sewers to serve the Industrial park, and replacement of approximately 1,000 linear feet of damaged gravity sewer lines within the city to improve inflow and infiltration. Wastewater treatment components included: multi-cell LEMNA system with advanced aeration, head works building, blower building and ultraviolet disinfection system. Financing of the $881,000 project was secured through a $500,000 CDBG grant, a $221,000 USDA-RD loan and a $160,000 USDA-RD grant.

There are many ways to measure past performance and the standards are different for each. However, we firmly hold that our actions that result in client satisfaction and ultimately, client retention, are the best measure of success. Our goal is to provide a quality project, on time and on budget.

Our Quality Control/Quality Assurance Team is comprised of principals/senior engineers who bring their years of combined experience and knowledge to the review table. Team Members: Kevin Garnett, PE, Executive Vice President; Donald Jenkins, PE, Executive Vice President; Ryan Huseman, PE, Senior Engineer; Scott E. Vogler, PE, Senior Principal Engineer; Max F. Middendorf, PE, Senior Principal Engineer; James D. Bensman, PE, SE, Senior Principal Engineer.

The leader of the QC/QA Team is determined by the engineering discipline and project scope of services. For this project, the leader will be Max F. Middendorf. Project concept, design plans and drawings, technical specifications, contract documents, and estimates of probable cost, project schedule and budget will be closely reviewed by Mr. Middendorf during the design phase. Construction phase will bring review of project progress to determine compliance with construction schedule/deadlines, review of change orders, quantities, pay requests, inspector's logs, and compliance with project budget and schedule. The majority of our business is repeat business. This is the direct result of satisfied customers.
